RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : PLAINTIFF HARBIN MANCHURIAN CUISINE, INCORPORATED'S Motion To Foreclose Upon Lien on the Dale Mak Family Limited Partnership Pursuant to Charging Orders is granted insofar as Plaintiff may foreclose on the judgment debtor's interests in the partnership, and their interests in the partnership's profits and surpluses may be sold at public auction. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-26-2010 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Ken A. Chan, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Harbin Manchurian Cuisine, Inc.
No appearance by defendants Da Bean Low, LLC, Dale Mak, Shirley Marie Mak or Danny Mak.
Default were entered against defendants on July 1, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, March 9, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Harbin Manchurian Cuisine, Inc., and against the Defaulted Defendants Da Bean Low, LLC, Dale Mak, Shirley Marie Mak and Danny Mak as follows:

Damages/Principal - $494,681.07.
Prejudgment Interest - Reserved.
Attorney's Fees - Reserved.
Costs - $565.00.

Total - $495,246.07.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Mar-02-2010 Continued To Mar-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Ken A. Chan, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Harbin Manchurian Cuisine, LLC.
No appearance by defendants Da Bean Low, LLC, Dale Mak, Shirley Marie Mak or Danny Mak.
Defaults were entered against defendants on July 1, 2009.
Doe defendants have not been dismissed.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was file on March 1, 2010.

The Court requires further information from plaintiff in order to proceed with today's hearing. Good cause appearing, this matter is continued to March 9, 2010 @ 9:00 a.m. in Department 218.
